title: The Design of Everyday Things
author: Don Norman
review: The bible of UX. Norman's concept of affordances fundamentally changed how I think about designing for humans.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
review: A sweeping narrative of humanity that makes you question every institution we take for granted.

title: The Creative Act
author: Rick Rubin
review: Less about music production, more about tuning into the universe. A meditation on creativity itself.

title: Zero to One
author: Peter Thiel
review: Contrarian thinking at its sharpest. Forces you to ask: are you building the future or just copying it?

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
review: A masterclass in human cognition. Every product decision I make now runs through this lens.

title: The Almanack of Naval Ravikant
author: Eric Jorgenson
review: More than a business book — it's a philosophy for living with intention, building wealth, and finding peace.

title: Show Your Work!
author: Austin Kleon
review: The permission slip I needed to build in public. Changed how I think about sharing the process.
